title: senecio eubaeus
intro: Senecio eubaeus, also known as Greek ironwort or mountain tea, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Greece. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids such as usnic acid, which has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Senecio eubaeus include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and even exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or tincture, with some studies also exploring its potential in topical creams and ointments for wound healing.
